page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmg9i.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 6982P-C cores operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, this server provides 192 vCPUs without hyperthreading, ensuring consistent execution performance for compute-heavy tasks.

The system is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 64 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, and high-performance computing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio. The lack of hyperthreading makes it particularly suitable for workloads sensitive to resource sharing, such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, and distributed databases.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmg9i.48xlarge is a high-capacity server featuring 192 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 6982P-C cores running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 192 vCPUs with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The server does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. This resource configuration is designed for large-scale general-purpose applications, multi-threaded compute tasks, and database workloads requiring dedicated physical processing units and high network throughput.

tagline: A dedicated 192-core Intel Xeon server with balanced memory and high-speed network connectivity for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
